llm_operation.reason: MindSpore ReduceMean does not accept 'params' as a keyword argument; instead, it expects positional or keyword arguments 'x' (input tensor) and 'axis' (int/tuple/list/Tensor), with 'keep_dims' as a constructor parameter. The original test case incorrectly wraps parameters under 'params' key, and also passes 'axis': [] which is invalid — MindSpore requires axis to be None, int, tuple, list, or Tensor, but empty list [] is not supported. Per documentation, default behavior (no axis specified) reduces all dimensions, equivalent to torch.mean(input) without dim. Also, keep_dims must be passed to the operator instance, not at call time. Therefore, we must construct ReduceMean with keep_dims=False and call it with only x (and optionally valid axis). Since PyTorch test case has no dim specified, we match by omitting axis entirely (i.e., reduce all dims), and ensure keep_dims is set in constructor.
